events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes block/brick laying where joints are not tooled concave, allowing water entry. This is a defect. Joints should be tooled concave to shed water and prevent moisture intrusion. This is non-compliant with TMS 402/602 which requires proper joint tooling.

  "findings": [
    "Mortar joints not tooled to a concave profile, leaving flush or poorly struck joints",
    "Untooled or improperly tooled joints allow water entry into the masonry assembly",
    "Joint profile does not meet weathering resistance requirements for exposed masonry"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rake and re-tool all exposed mortar joints to a concave profile while mortar is still workable",
    "If mortar has set, grind out joints to sufficient depth and repoint with concave tooling",
    "Inspect completed joints for consistent compression and weather-shedding profile"
  ],
  "rationale": "Concave tooling compresses the mortar surface and sheds water; flush or unstruck joints are a known water-intrusion defect and violate TMS 402/602 workmanship and durability provisions for exposed masonry.",
  "references": ["TMS 402/602"]
}
